video_title: The REAL reason the US can’t beat Iran

Even worse, this land east of Iran is strategic high ground. They call it the key to India, meaning if Russia got access here, it would threaten the British Empire's most valuable colony. Iran might take this over and give Russia access. This would be a nightmare for Britain. A British general calls Russia a, quote, "disagreeable and even dangerous neighbor." So the British Empire jumps into a competition for influence in this region that would come to be known as the Great Game. The war ravages Iran's economy and at least two million Iranians die from food shortages and disease caused by the war. Once again, Iranians are paying the price while the empires fight to protect their wealth and influence. After the First World War, Russia is going through a communist revolution turning into the USSR or Soviet Union and they leave Iran. But they're just next door. They could come back at any time. But for now it's just the British Empire who wants to increase their influence here even more due to the vast oil reserves they're discovering. Oil had been a game-changer in World War I. It's one of the reasons the British won, and Germany, who didn't have access to nearly as much oil, lost. Oil is a strategic weapon. They can't leave Iran. But Iranians are fed up with what has been over a hundred years of foreigners running their country from the outside. So this military man rises up. (subdued music) He works with the parliament to throw out the old Shah and promises to make Iran great again, trying to get the people to remember that this was a superpower. They need to restore that greatness. His name was Reza Shah. At a time of rising nationalism everywhere, Reza Shah emphasizes Persian identity and greatness, reminding people that the majority of Iranians speak Farsi, not Arabic like much of the Middle East. And this Shah says that Iran was great before the Arabs brought Islam to Iran. So he wants to reduce the influence of religion in the society. He wants to make the secular state the only political and legal power. In 1936, he bans women from wearing any kind of cover or veil. He wants to modernize Iran and push back against all the humiliating foreign influence that has been stopping the country from being able to determine their own destiny for decades. It feels like a new era for the country. He's building hospitals and schools and roads and a university. Farsi becomes the official language. Nomads are forced to settle down. He builds a railway across the country. He builds a strong centralized bureaucracy, a secular court system, a powerful army, and he demands that outsiders call it Iran instead of Persia. And he crushes anyone who opposes his modernizing efforts. Okay, but not too fast. This is where we're gonna see that pattern again. As part of all of this modernizing effort, the new leader brings in German engineers. And look at the date. Back in Europe, Germany just started a war with its neighbors. This is World War II. (serious music) So here comes Russia and Britain again. They demand that Reza Shah expel the Germans who could easily sabotage these weapon supply lines that the Soviets need to fight back against Germany in Europe, or maybe they could sabotage Britain's valuable source of fuel for the war. Reza Shah doesn't want to give into the big empires. He's done with that. Okay, so World War II is over and I want you to pay attention to this guy, Mohammad Reza Shah. Yeah, he's a big part of the next chapter of this story, so just keep an eye on him. Oh, and let's update his photo. He's not 21 anymore. World War II is over.
